mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are negative in >55% of rate-cut months over a 5-year walk-forward test.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and falling consumer spending, which reduces corporate earnings expectations and drives negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns are positive in >60% of instances where the unemployment rate crosses the 0.3pp threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in retail sales and housing starts indicate synchronized consumer and business demand expansion, which boosts corporate revenue forecasts and produces positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive returns when both series rise concurrently, with a Rank IC below 0.02 over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in the industrial production index reflects weakening manufacturing output and supply-side contraction, which immediately pressures equity valuations and generates negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are positive in >50% of months where industrial production declines, regardless of inflation levels.

mechanism_narrative: When the consumer price index exceeds 245, persistent inflation erodes real corporate profits and forces tighter financial conditions, resulting in negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Returns are positive in >55% of months where CPIAUCSL remains above 245.

mechanism_narrative: Steady growth in total nonfarm payrolls expands the labor force and supports aggregate demand, which improves corporate earnings outlooks and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are negative in >50% of months with positive payroll growth over a 10-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between declining real GDP growth and falling retail sales indicates a synchronized demand contraction, which reduces equity valuations and produces negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The divergence signal yields positive forward returns in >60% of observed quarters, or the interaction term loses statistical significance in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production above 105 signals robust manufacturing capacity utilization and strong economic momentum, which immediately lifts equity prices and generates positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are negative in >55% of months where the industrial production index exceeds 105.
